assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 12: 'Ecosystem | RPM' and lines 31-32: 'Package: openssl-libs (system RPM package)' / 'Ecosystem: **RPM**'. The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM, not Cargo, based on the openssl-libs package name.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 8-12 show all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). The dependency chain context (line 26) confirms rpms.lock.yaml was used: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present -> explicit install'.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 26-27: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present -> explicit install (openssl-libs listed in rpms.lock.yaml)' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The origin is classified as explicit install, not base image.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: For the 2.2.x stream (lines 5-9), no remediation task is needed because the fix is already present in 2.2.3+. For the 2.1.x cross-stream (lines 66-124), a single preemptive task is created for the Konflux release repo 'rhtpa-release.0.3.z' (line 82). No upstream backport + downstream propagation two-task flow is used. The single-task system package path is followed.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 79-124 contain the preemptive task description with all required sections: '## Repository' (line 80: rhtpa-release.0.3.z), '## Target Branch' (line 84: main), '## Description' (lines 88-101), '## Implementation Notes' (lines 104-110), '## Acceptance Criteria' (lines 112-114). Additional sections '## Test Requirements' and '## Dependencies' are also present. No '## Files to Modify' section exists, which is correct per remediation-templates.md line 186: 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.'

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context includes an SBOM verification line — either showing the cosign comparison result or noting that SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 24-31 contain the Dependency Chain Context section. It includes only: rpms.lock.yaml presence check, origin classification ('explicit install'), and remediation guidance. There is no SBOM verification line — no mention of cosign, SBOM, or any indication that SBOM verification was performed or skipped.
